title: Light and dark mode
question: Should the viewer offer a light theme, and how should the theme be chosen and remembered?
rationale: Add a light theme with a sun/moon toggle; it starts dark, follows the computer's setting on first open, then remembers your pick and applies it before draw so there's no flash. Chosen over auto-only (no override) or dark-only (hard to read in bright light).

options.label: Light and dark with a toggle (starts dark, follows the computer at first, remembers your pick)

tradeoffs.note: a sun/moon toggle in the top bar, remembered next time
follows the computer's light/dark setting until you choose
a second color set plus fixing a few too-pale text colors

options.label: Auto only (follow the computer, no toggle)

tradeoffs.note: can't override the computer's setting just for this viewer
no toggle and nothing to remember
no light option for daylight or glare
